# core


<!-- WARNING: THIS FILE WAS AUTOGENERATED! DO NOT EDIT! -->

# Base Variables

These variables contain the basic orderings for Western music theory.

# Note

The [`Note`](https://CarloLepelaars.github.io/musy/core.html#note) is
the basic atomic unit in music. Combinations of notes will form chords
and scales. Notes and chords together will form songs.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/CarloLepelaars/musy/blob/master/musy/core.py#L20"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### Note

>  Note (note:str)

*Base class for objects needing a basic `__repr__`*

``` python
a_sharp = Note("A#")
a_sharp
```

    Note(note='A#')

``` python
assert str(a_sharp) == "A#"
a_sharp.note
```

    'A#'

``` python
c_sharp = Note("C#")
c_sharp
```

    Note(note='C#')

``` python
assert Note("E#") == "F"
assert Note("B#") == "C"
assert Note("C##") == "D"
assert Note("Fb") == "E"
assert Note("Abb") == "G"
assert Note("Bbb") == "A"
assert Note("Cb") == "B"
assert Note("C") == Note("C")
assert Note("E#") == Note("F")
assert Note("A#") != Note("B")
```

## Adding to Note

Adding semitones to a note will return a new note with n semitones added
above the original note.

For example, adding 1 semitone to A# will return B.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/CarloLepelaars/musy/blob/master/musy/core.py#L46"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### Note.\_\_add\_\_

>  Note.__add__ (semitones:int)

*Add n semitones to a note.*

``` python
for i in range(1, 13):
    print(a_sharp+i)
```

    B
    C
    C#
    D
    D#
    E
    F
    F#
    G
    G#
    A
    A#

``` python
assert str(a_sharp+1) == "B"
assert str(a_sharp+11) == "A"
assert str(a_sharp+12) == "A#"
assert str(a_sharp+13) == "B"
```

## Subtracting from Note

Subtracting semitones from a note will return a new note with `n`
semitones subtracted from the original note.

For example, subtracting 1 semitone from C will return B. subtracting 1
semitone from A# will return A.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/CarloLepelaars/musy/blob/master/musy/core.py#L52"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### Note.\_\_sub\_\_

>  Note.__sub__ (semitones:int)

``` python
for i in range(1, 13):
    print(a_sharp-i)
```

    A
    G#
    G
    F#
    F
    E
    D#
    D
    C#
    C
    B
    A#

``` python
assert str(a_sharp-1) == "A"
assert str(a_sharp-11) == "B"
assert str(a_sharp-12) == "A#"
assert str(a_sharp-13) == "A"
```

## Augment

Augmenting adds a sharp to a note.

For example, augmenting A# will return B.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/CarloLepelaars/musy/blob/master/musy/core.py#L57"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### Note.augment

>  Note.augment ()

``` python
assert str(a_sharp.augment()) == "B"
a_sharp.augment()
```

    Note(note='B')

`augment` operations can be chained together.

``` python
assert str(a_sharp.augment().augment().augment().augment()) == "D"
a_sharp.augment().augment().augment().augment()
```

    Note(note='D')

## Diminish

Diminishing a note is the inverse of augmenting a note and removes a
sharp from a note.

For example, diminishing A# will return A.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/CarloLepelaars/musy/blob/master/musy/core.py#L62"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### Note.diminish

>  Note.diminish ()

``` python
assert str(a_sharp.diminish()) == "A"
a_sharp.diminish()
```

    Note(note='A')

As with `augment`, `diminish` operations can be chained together.

``` python
assert str(a_sharp.diminish().diminish()) == "Ab"
a_sharp.diminish().diminish()
```

    Note(note='Ab')

Lastly, `augment` and `diminish` can be chained together.

``` python
assert str(a_sharp.diminish().augment()) == str(a_sharp)
a_sharp.diminish().augment()
```

    Note(note='A#')

## Interval

Two notes can be combined to form an interval. `interval` returns the
name of the interval between two notes.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/CarloLepelaars/musy/blob/master/musy/core.py#L67"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### Note.interval

>  Note.interval (other:__main__.Note, short=False)

The intervals between `A#` and `C#` is a minor third (b3).

``` python
c_sharp = Note("C#")
interval = a_sharp.interval(c_sharp)
assert interval == "minor third"
interval
```

    'minor third'

Shorthand can be retrieved from `interval` by setting `short=True`.

``` python
short_interval = a_sharp.interval(c_sharp, short=True)
assert short_interval == "b3"
short_interval
```

    'b3'

## Convert to Major or Minor

A note can be converted to its relative major or minor. How this is
converted can be visualized on the circle of fifths.

<img src="https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/3/33/Circle_of_fifths_deluxe_4.svg" width="40%" alt="Circle of Fifths">

For example, the relative minor of C is A. The relative major of C# is
E.

`minor` converts an arbitrary note to its relative minor. This is done
by subtracting 3 semitones from the note.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/CarloLepelaars/musy/blob/master/musy/core.py#L72"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### Note.minor

>  Note.minor ()

``` python
c = Note("C")
assert str(c.minor()) == "A"
c.minor()
```

    Note(note='A')

`major` converts an arbitrary note to its relative major. We add 3
semitones to the note.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/CarloLepelaars/musy/blob/master/musy/core.py#L76"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### Note.major

>  Note.major ()

``` python
assert str(c_sharp.major()) == "E"
c_sharp.major()
```

    Note(note='E')
